channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 and Tier 2 sources

item.title: Chinese propagandists stoking dissent over Trump tariffs and data centres, OpenAI says
item.description: OpenAI reports that Chinese-speaking users were asked to use ChatGPT to create slogans and cartoons criticizing the US president's trade policies, highlighting ongoing influence operations.

item.title: Pentagon's expanded blacklist of Chinese firms tests Xi's US visit
item.description: Washington's latest move highlights intense competition between the US and China, testing the fragile "stability" reached at the leadership summit in Beijing.

item.title: Iran declares closure of Hormuz, threatens to attack ships in strait
item.description: Iran announced the closure of the strategic Strait of Hormuz after latest US strikes, threatening to attack any ships passing through the critical waterway.

item.title: Massive new US strikes rock multiple targets inside Iran
item.description: Iranian state media networks confirm huge explosions across Bandar Abbas, Sirik and the nearby city of Minab in the southern part of the country amid a new wave of US attacks.

item.title: Explosions and airstrikes reported across southern Iran amid new US attacks
item.description: Explosions and airstrikes were reported across southern Iran early Thursday amid a new wave of US attacks, according to local reports.

item.title: Ukraine police chief: Russia recruits young women to kill Ukrainian servicemen
item.description: Ukraines police chief accused Russia of recruiting teenage Ukrainian girls to assassinate military personnel, following the arrest of a 17-year-old suspected of murdering a serviceman on instructions from a Russian operative.

item.title: China-North Korea claim wins from Xi visit, but limits remain
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinpings visit to North Korea elevated Kim Jong Uns global stature and pulled Pyongyang closer into Beijings orbit, though analysts note significant limits remain in the bilateral relationship.

item.title: Trump sticks with Pulte for intel job as risk grows of lapse in spy powers
item.description: A lapse in US foreign intelligence-gathering powers has grown more likely as President Trump continues to back his nominee for the intelligence post amid congressional gridlock.

item.title: Israeli Airstrikes Kill 13 in Southern Lebanon as Hezbollah Conflict Escalates
item.description: Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon killed at least 13 people on Wednesday, Lebanese security sources said, as Israel pressed its campaign against Hezbollah, with the Iran-backed group claiming fresh attacks against Israeli forces in the south.

item.title: US Inflation Hits Three-Year High at 4.2% as Iran War Drives Energy Prices Higher
item.description: Consumer prices rose at the fastest pace in three years in May, reaching 4.2% annually, as the Middle East conflict sent energy costs surging and gave the Federal Reserve ammunition to keep interest rates unchanged into 2027.

item.title: UN Nuclear Watchdog Demands Iran Report Enriched Uranium Stocks
item.description: The IAEA's 35-nation Board of Governors passed a US-backed resolution telling Iran to declare its remaining enriched uranium stocks and allow inspectors to verify them, a move that could complicate Washington's ongoing talks with Tehran.

item.title: Erdogan warns Israel's attacks on Syria, Lebanon now threaten Turkey
item.description: Turkish President Erdogan stated that Israel's military operations in Syria and Lebanon have escalated to the point where they threaten Turkey itself, calling Israel's aggression a threat to the whole world.

item.title: Ukraine partners with Taurus missile maker for deep strike systems
item.description: Ukrainian defense company Ukrainian Armor and European firm MBDA initiated a strategic partnership to develop deep strike capabilities, enhancing Ukraine's indigenous defense production.

item.title: Countries vie to become energy safe haven as Hormuz crisis deepens
item.description: Multiple nations are pitching the Trump administration on energy alternatives as the Strait of Hormuz remains disrupted, seeking to position themselves as secure energy suppliers.

item.title: Ireland sets online safety, security priorities for EU presidency
item.description: Ireland's EU presidency will focus on improving online safety for children and boosting Europe's economic competitiveness and security amid growing geopolitical challenges.

item.title: Bank of Japan Governor Ueda Hospitalised, Will Miss Key Policy Meeting
item.description: BOJ Governor Kazuo Ueda has been hospitalised for medical treatment and will miss the June 15-16 policy meeting, the central bank announced, raising uncertainty about Japans monetary policy direction during a critical period.

item.title: Israel Kills 16 in Lebanon as UN Announces International Law Probe
item.description: Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon killed at least 16 people as UN investigators announced they will arrive next week to assess potential violations of international law by all parties in the escalating conflict.

item.title: Trump Says He Loves Inflation as Prices Hit 3-Year High
item.description: President Donald Trump dismissed concerns about surging consumer prices, saying I love the inflation and claiming the U.S. is taking millions of barrels of Iranian oil, as inflation reached its highest level in three years.

item.title: Violent Unrest Erupts in Belfast Over Knife Attack Arrest
item.description: Police in Northern Ireland used water cannons against protesters as violent unrest flared in Belfast following the arrest of a Sudanese man charged with attempted murder in a stabbing attack, with officials calling for calm.

item.title: NATO Forward Land Forces Stand Up New Multinational Battlegroup in Finland
item.description: NATO has stood up its Forward Land Forces Finland during a ceremony at the Swedish Army base, establishing the alliances newest multinational battlegroup as part of its enhanced deterrence posture in Northern Europe.

item.title: Trump Threatens New Military Action Against Iran After Helicopter Downing
item.description: President Trump warned of additional strikes on Iran, accusing Tehran of "playing us for suckers" after a US helicopter was downed near the Strait of Hormuz. The escalation comes amid ongoing tensions in the Middle East.

item.title: China Re-centers North Korea Ties as Nuclear Silence Reshapes Regional Balance
item.description: As Xi Jinping visits Pyongyang, China is reasserting its influence with North Korea at a time when Kim Jong Un has remained silent on nuclear activities. The diplomatic move signals Beijing effort to counter US pressure in the region.

item.title: US Navy Establishes Naval Support Activity in Western Australia Under AUKUS Pact
item.description: As part of the AUKUS security pact, NSA Stirling will provide support for personnel assigned to Submarine Rotational Forces, strengthening Western military presence in the Indo-Pacific.

item.title: Mariupol Port No Longer Operational After Ukrainian Strikes, Azov Corps Says
item.description: Russian-occupied Mariupol's port has been rendered non-operational by Ukrainian strikes, a significant blow to Russian logistics in the Azov Sea region.

item.title: ICEYE and Rheinmetall Form German Joint Venture for Space-Based ISR
item.description: Finnish SAR satellite company ICEYE and German defense giant Rheinmetall have launched a joint venture for space-based intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance capabilities.
